mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate cuts are not significantly positive.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 9.0%, labor market weakness signals severe demand destruction, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 9.0%, or out-of-sample accuracy drops below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real personal income combined with falling industrial production indicates inventory buildup and weakening demand, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Joint signal fails to predict negative returns with > 55% accuracy over a 5-year walk-forward test, or interaction term loses statistical significance.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during periods of low inflation signals robust economic growth without pricing pressure, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Signal predicts positive returns in < 52% of cases during low-inflation regimes, or Rank IC turns negative when CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 3% annually.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in housing starts reflect improving consumer confidence and construction sector activity, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following housing starts increases are not significantly higher than baseline, or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When core inflation measured by CPIAUCSL rises above 2.5%, anticipated monetary tightening compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 60-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or the threshold fails to separate return distributions significantly.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls alongside declining unemployment rates indicate a strengthening labor market that supports corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Combined labor signal predicts positive returns in < 53% of cases, or interaction effect vanishes in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of negative GDP growth, rising real personal income signals resilient consumer spending that cushions equity markets, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Signal fails to generate positive 5-day returns in > 55% of recessionary windows, or predictive power disappears when GDP growth turns positive.
